evals.prompt: Create a simple platformer game with 3 levels
evals.expected_output: A Scratch platformer game with player movement, platforms to jump on, and 3 distinct levels
expectations: The project contains a player sprite with movement controls
The project includes variables for score or lives
The project has at least 2 sprites (player and platform/obstacle)
The project uses motion blocks (move, turn, or go to)
The project uses control blocks (forever, if, or repeat)
The .sb3 file is created in the output directory

evals.prompt: Build a Pong game with two paddles and a ball
evals.expected_output: A classic Pong game with two paddles, a bouncing ball, and scoring
expectations: The project has at least 3 sprites (ball and 2 paddles)
The ball has movement scripts using motion blocks
The project includes variables for scoring
The paddles respond to key presses
The ball bounces when hitting edges
The .sb3 file is created in the output directory

evals.prompt: Make an animation of a cat dancing with music
evals.expected_output: An animated Scratch project showing a cat with costume-switching dance moves
expectations: The project uses the cat sprite with multiple costumes
The animation uses costume switching blocks
The animation uses motion blocks (turn, move, or glide)
The project uses timing blocks (wait)
The animation runs when the green flag is clicked
The .sb3 file is created in the output directory

evals.prompt: Create a drawing program where you can change colors and clear the screen
evals.expected_output: An interactive drawing program with pen tools and color selection buttons
expectations: The project uses the pen extension (pen down/up, clear)
The project has multiple button sprites for color selection
The drawing responds to mouse movement
There's a clear/erase button
Buttons respond to click events
The .sb3 file is created in the output directory

evals.prompt: Build a quiz game with 5 questions about general knowledge
evals.expected_output: An interactive quiz game that asks questions and tracks correct answers
expectations: The project uses ask/answer blocks for user input
The project uses variables to track score
The project uses conditional logic (if) to check answers
The project uses broadcasting to communicate between sprites
The project has at least one sprite for the quiz host/character
The .sb3 file is created in the output directory

evals.prompt: Make a simple catch game where you catch falling objects
evals.expected_output: A game where a basket or character moves to catch falling objects while avoiding misses
expectations: The player sprite moves with keyboard or mouse
Falling objects use motion blocks and loops
The project detects when objects are caught (touching)
The project includes a score variable
The game has a game over or restart condition
The .sb3 file is created in the output directory
